en.name: Arterial blood specimen
en.longDescription: ## Definition & Clinical Context Arterial blood specimen is a sample of blood obtained by puncture or catheterisation of a systemic artery (radial, femoral, brachial). It is used primarily for blood gas analysis (PaO2, PaCO2, pH, SaO2, lactate) and reflects the oxygen and carbon-dioxide status of blood after gas exchange in the lungs and before tissue extraction. Arterial sampling is more painful and carries a higher risk of complications than venous sampling, so it is reserved for clinical questions where the arterial-venous difference matters. **OMOP CDM usage.** This concept set targets the `SPECIMEN.specimen_concept_id` field. In OMOP CDM v5.4, a MEASUREMENT row is linked to its SPECIMEN row via `measurement_event_id` (set to `specimen_id`) and `meas_event_field_concept_id` (a concept pointing to the SPECIMEN table); the older `FACT_RELATIONSHIP` table can also carry the link. In practice, many OMOP datasets do not materialise a SPECIMEN row and rely on the analyte-side LOINC (`Lactate [Moles/volume] in Arterial blood`) to convey the specimen, since the specimen is already encoded in the LOINC name. This concept set is intended for datasets that explicitly model specimens. ## Included Concepts The set is anchored on the SNOMED Specimen concept `Arterial blood specimen`, expanded to include all its descendants. The set resolves to one specimen: - *4047496 / 122552005 — Arterial blood specimen (SNOMED)* ## Excluded Concepts The following SNOMED Specimen descendant is deliberately excluded by name: - *45766301 / 703430008 — Arterial cord blood specimen*: cord blood is physiologically distinct from post-natal arterial blood (placental circulation, oxygenation pattern). ## Mapping Notes - **Default mapping** when the source data records a sample collected from an artery (radial line, femoral puncture, arterial blood gas): `4047496 / 122552005 — Arterial blood specimen`. - **Source records umbilical / cord sampling**: do **not** map. Out of scope. - **Source records a venous or capillary sample**: use the appropriate venous / peripheral / whole blood specimen concept set. ## References [1] SNOMED International. SNOMED CT — Arterial blood specimen (122552005). Available via OHDSI Athena: https://athena.ohdsi.org/search-terms/terms/4047496
